news_title: Product safety recall - Certain 2026 Ford transit, Ford transit custom and Ford Tourneo custom vehicles - The National Consumer Commission (NCC)
efet_title: Product safety recall - Certain 2026 Ford transit, Ford transit custom and Ford Tourneo custom vehicles - The National Consumer Commission
efet_body:
Product safety recall – Certain 2026 Ford transit, Ford transit custom and Ford Tourneo custom vehicles
Date Posted:
July 15, 2026
The National Consumer Commission (NCC) informs consumers of a product safety recall issued by Ford Motor Company of Southern Africa (Manufacturing) (Pty) Ltd. The recall affects certain 2026 model year Ford Transit, Ford Transit Custom and Ford Tourneo Custom vehicles. The affected vehicles were sold from 7 October 2025 to 9 June 2026 in South Africa and were also exported to Namibia, Botswana, Lesotho and Swaziland.
According to the supplier, the affected vehicles are equipped with an 8F57 automatic transmission, which may contain an Electronic One-Way Clutch Assembly built with an unseated snap ring. This may result in a sudden and unexpected loss of vehicle movement. When the vehicle is shifted into drive, it may fail to move forward due to a loss of first gear. Similarly, the vehicle may lose the ability to operate in reverse.
Consumers who own the affected vehicles are urged to take their vehicles to the nearest authorised Ford dealership to arrange for an inspection and repair. Any remedial work will be carried out at no cost to the consumer.
Questions relating to this recall may be directed to:
ProductRecall@thencc.org.za
.
Ends
Issued by: National Consumer Commission (NCC)
For media enquiries, contact: Phetho Ntaba (Spokesperson)
082 809 2031/012 065 2040
Follow us on:
Facebook: The National Consumer Commission
LinkedIn: National Consumer Commission
X Handle: @TheNCC_RSA
WhatsApp Channel: @TheNCC_RSA

news_title: SPAR recalls 11 yoghurt products due to health risk - The Citizen
efet_title: Media Statement: Product Safety Recall - Top Score Instant Porridge by SPAR Group - The National Consumer Commission
efet_body:
Media Statement: Product Safety Recall – Top Score Instant Porridge by SPAR Group
Date Posted:
October 1, 2024
On 30 September 2024, the National Consumer Commission (NCC) received an urgent precautionary product safety recall notification of the Top Score Instant Porridge brand produced in Namibia and supplied to the Spar Group in South Africa. Namib Mills, based in Namibia, notified the NCC of an urgent precautionary recall of this product and the local supplier, Spar Group, has also communicated this information to the NCC.
South African consumers who purchased Top Score Instant Porridge by SPAR Group are urged to stop consuming the product and immediately return it to the retailer for a full refund.
South African consumers who purchased the product are urged to stop consuming the product and immediately return it to the retailer for a full refund.
There is currently a separate investigation under way, including laboratory analysis, by relevant authorities in the Buffalo City Metro, where safety concerns of the product were first raised. The NCC awaits the outcome of this investigation as it will inform the steps the NCC can take in terms of the Consumer Protection Act.
In the interim, the NCC is separately investigating the product recall, especially given the potential public health concerns. The NCC is working closely with other authorities that are investigating this product recall.
NCC’s Acting Commissioner Mr Hardin Ratshisusu says: “Consumers are urged to immediately return the affected product as its further use may be harmful. It is also the responsibility of the concerned retailers to take every step possible to ensure the product is removed from the shelves and is not distributed further.”
Background information on the product recall
The recalled product was produced on 06/08/2024, with the best-before date of 06/02/2026
While the manufacturer Namib Mills communicated the recall of the Instant Vanilla Flavoured Maize meal, Spar Group South Africa has communicated other flavours (see attached).
The NCC is monitoring this recall in terms of section 60 of the Consumer Protection Act (No 68 of 2008) read with the Product Recall Safety Guidelines.
ENDS
See full Media Statement below:

news_title: National Consumer Commission recalls a range of SPAR yoghurt products - EWN
efet_title: Media Statement: Deli Hummus range Product Safety Recall - The National Consumer Commission
efet_body:
Media Statement: Deli Hummus range Product Safety Recall
Date Posted:
October 3, 2024
On 16 September 2024, Shoprite Checkers notified the Consumer Commission (NCC) of a product recall of the Deli Hummus range manufactured by BM Foods (a member of Sea Harvest Group), following the detection of Listeria Monocytogenes which is a species of foodborne bacteria.
The affected products are the Deli Hummus range, specifically the following:
Traditional Hummus (125g and 300g);
Reduced Fat Hummus (125g);
Zataar Hummus (125g and 300g);
Red Pepper Hummus (125g and 300g); and
Caramelised Onion Hummus (125g).
The respective sell-by dates of the products are 10 September 2024 to 08 October 2024.
Subsequent to the product recall notification, and in line with Section 60 of the Consumer Protection Act 68 of 2008, Shoprite Checkers has now provided an interim Investigation Report to the NCC on the Deli Hummus range. The preliminary findings of the report point to some areas of concern on product handling at the manufacturing level, however, the NCC awaits a final report from Shoprite Checkers. Parallel to the said in-house processes, the NCC is independently investigating the recalled products on identified operational issues that pertain to food safety.
The NCC’s Acting Commissioner, Mr. Hardin Ratshisusu, urged consumers to immediately stop consuming the products and return same to the supplier for a full refund with or without any proof of purchase. He further added that “at this stage, the NCC is not able to determine the extent of the distribution of the products as this is the subject of an investigation. Suppliers are urged not to distribute these products further until the investigation is finalised.”
The NCC is monitoring this recall based on the Product Recall provisions of the Consumer Protection Action read with the product recall safety guidelines.
ENDS
Issued by: National Consumer Commission (NCC)
Share on Social Media:
Media Centre

Speeches

news_title: Consumer Commission issues recall alert of various SPAR yoghurts - SABC News
efet_body:
Consumer Alerts
Welcome to The National Consumer Commission’s “Consumer Alerts” page. Here, you’ll find timely information that safeguards your consumer rights and interests.
Consumer Alert: Procurement of School Uniforms & Other Learning-Related Goods & Services
Consumer Alert: Beware of Pyramid Schemes
Consumer Alert on Festive Season Sales
Consumer Alert: Beware of Festive Season Scams
Consumer Alert: Shopping Online
Consumer Alert: Defective Goods, Rights and Recourse
Consumer Alert: Enterprise ready-to-eat Food Products
Consumer Alert: COVID 19
Consumer Alert: Panic Buying
Consumer Alerts – Stay Protected, Stay Informed
Welcome to The National Consumer Commission’s “Consumer Alerts” page. Here, you’ll find timely information that safeguards your consumer rights and interests.
Consumer Alerts are also crucial notifications from us, keeping you informed about emerging trends, risks, and marketplace issues. Covering recalls, scams, and additionally, market developments, they impact consumers.
Regarding Consumer Alerts
These alerts further matter because they protect you from unsafe products, fraudulent schemes, and other threats. Indeed, they empower you, ensuring you make informed choices and take preventive measures.
Similarly, our alerts are swift, issued as soon as we identify a potential issue, delivering essential information when you need it most. They are clear, offering concise insights for your understanding and action.
Moreover, to make the most of our Consumer Alerts, stay informed by checking regularly, take action following recommendations, and share alerts with friends and family.
At The National Consumer Commission, we believe an informed consumer is an empowered one. Again, our alerts exemplify our commitment to your well-being and protection. Explore them, stay vigilant, and make informed choices in today’s dynamic consumer landscape.
Learn more about What we Do to notify the public or, alternatively, Contact Us for information.
